Title: Oxidative rearrangement of (+)-sesamin by CYP92B14 co-generates twin dietary lignans in sesame

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Prime target for metabolic engineering or marker-assisted selection to increase sesame lignan diversity and dietary value. Functional CYP92B14 can be selected to enhance (+)-sesamolin production, while stacking/co-expressing CYP92B14 with CYP81Q1 is recommended for efficient heterologous production of (+)-sesamolin and (+)-sesaminol from (+)-pinoresinol. Editing should avoid C-terminal disruption because loss of the last four residues abolishes activity. Deployment should consider that sesamolin-deficient lines can still accumulate sesaminol glucosides in vivo, implying additional enzymes may contribute to sesaminol formation.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidizes (+)-sesamin to co-generate (+)-sesamolin and (+)-sesaminol. The sesamolin branch proceeds through a novel oxidative rearrangement of alpha-oxy-substituted aryl groups (ORA) with oxygen insertion between the furan and aromatic rings; sesaminol is produced both through ORA and by direct aromatic-ring oxygenation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes conversion of (+)-sesamin into the twin dietary lignans (+)-sesamolin and (+)-sesaminol; loss-of-function Del4C allele is associated with sesamolin deficiency in sesame seeds.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Genetic mapping of sesamolin-deficient RILs; RNA-seq expression profiling; heterologous expression in yeast; microsome enzyme assays; CO-difference spectroscopy; immunoblotting; stable-isotope labeling with LC-MS/NMR; mutant allele comparison (Del4C vs full length); metabolite profiling in seeds and yeast.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Seed developmental stage; in vitro microsome assays with NADPH; isotope-labeling conditions with 18O2 or H2 18O for mechanistic interpretation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expression increased during mid-stage seed development and coincided with lignan accumulation; highly expressed in developing seeds.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: In sesame developing seeds, especially mid-stage seed maturation.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq, NCBI SRA PRJNA350858; RAD-Seq for genetic mapping (accession not provided)
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: A thymine insertion in accession #4294/Maruemon causes a Del4C mutant lacking the last four C-terminal amino acids and abolishes activity. CYP92B14 is the first identified enzyme mediating ORA chemistry. Product ratio inferred from labeling/NMR was approximately sesamolin via ORA : sesaminol via ORA : sesaminol via direct oxygenation = 72:17:11. Sesamol and (+)-samin behaved as intermediates/inhibitory side products. Sesamolin-deficient RILs still accumulated sesaminol triglucoside in vivo, suggesting other enzymes may also contribute to sesaminol biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Terminal sesame lignan branch from (+)-sesamin to (+)-sesamolin/(+)-sesaminol
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Functional deficiency leads to loss of sesamolin accumulation and increased upstream (+)-sesamin accumulation. Possible flux redistribution without major developmental penalties reported.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Terminal determinant of sesamolin biosynthesis; key control step for final lignan diversification
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP92B14 was genetically linked to sesamolin production and the inactive Del4C allele co-segregated with sesamolin deficiency. 2. Recombinant CYP92B14 directly converted (+)-sesamin into (+)-sesamolin and (+)-sesaminol in vitro. 3. Stable-isotope studies showed CYP92B14 performs a novel ORA reaction and also direct aromatic oxygenation. 4. Co-expression with CYP81Q1 enhanced CYP92B14 activity, completing the final steps of major sesame lignan biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Seeds, especially developing and mature sesame seeds
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Dietary lignans with antioxidant and health-promoting activities; implicated in plant specialized-metabolite diversity and possible defense functions.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Sesame cv. Masekin; heterologous yeast INVsc strain
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful upstream pathway enzyme for pathway reconstruction and stacking with CYP92B14 to produce valuable dietary lignans in heterologous systems. In breeding/engineering, CYP81Q1 should be maintained or co-introduced when aiming to channel flux from (+)-pinoresinol toward (+)-sesamin, (+)-sesamolin, and (+)-sesaminol.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Sequentially oxygenates (+)-pinoresinol via (+)-piperitol to form (+)-sesamin by constructing two methylenedioxy bridges.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supplies the direct precursor (+)-sesamin and functionally cooperates with CYP92B14, enhancing production of (+)-sesamolin and (+)-sesaminol in yeast co-expression assays.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Prior biochemical characterization cited in paper; expression profiling in developing seeds; yeast co-expression/bioassay with CYP92B14 and CPR1 showing sequential conversion from (+)-pinoresinol to downstream lignans; substrate feeding assays.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Seed developmental stage; yeast substrate-feeding assays with (+)-pinoresinol and (+)-sesamin
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed during seed development with a profile coinciding with CYP92B14.
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Midstream to downstream, immediately upstream of CYP92B14 branch
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: CYP81Q1 alone did not react with (+)-sesamin in this study, but co-expression with CYP92B14 increased consumption of (+)-sesamin and production of (+)-sesamolin/(+)-sesaminol, implying functional coordination or metabolon-like interaction.
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Conversion of (+)-pinoresinol to (+)-sesamin upstream of terminal lignan diversification
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No direct penalties reported; altering CYP81Q1 likely shifts flux among upstream and downstream lignans.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key upstream control step for sesamin supply; not established here as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP81Q1 is the established sesamin synthase producing (+)-sesamin from (+)-pinoresinol via (+)-piperitol. 2. Its expression profile overlaps with CYP92B14 during seed development. 3. Co-expression of CYP81Q1 with CYP92B14 and CPR1 in yeast enabled sequential biosynthesis of (+)-sesaminol and (+)-sesamolin from (+)-pinoresinol and enhanced downstream product formation from (+)-sesamin.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Dietary lignans with antioxidant and health-promoting properties.
